summary: Deep research + adversarial verification for P2 gap partition 4 (21 models)
logs: research: 23 models researched, 0 rejected as unconfirmable
verify: 23 models confirmed, 0 rejected by verification

models.name: Amazon Titan Text (Express & Lite)
models.archDisplay: Dense transformer (parameter count undisclosed)

price.provider: AWS Bedrock (Titan Text Express; Titan Text Lite is $0.30/$0.40 per M tokens)

models.note: Amazon's original Bedrock-native text duo predating Nova: Express (8K ctx, English + 100 languages in preview) and Lite (4K ctx, English-only, tuned for fine-tuning).
models.verificationNotes: Confirmed real via AWS 'What's New' post (GA 2023-11-29, re:Invent 2023) and independently via SiliconANGLE coverage. Confirmed Express: 8K context, English + 100 languages in preview, $0.80/$1.60 per M tokens ($0.0008/$0.0016 per 1K). Confirmed Lite: 4K context, English-only, tuned for fine-tuning, $0.30/$0.40 per M tokens. All values match; no changes. Note: record intentionally combines two variants into one row using Express's spec as primary (ctxK=8); this is a simplification but not an error, and is disclosed in the note/price fields.

models.name: Amazon Nova Lite
models.archDisplay: Transformer, architecture/size undisclosed
models.note: Low-cost multimodal (text/image/video-in) Nova tier tuned for speed; launched alongside Micro and Pro at re:Invent 2024.

results.notes: 0-shot CoT; figure traces to Amazon's Nova technical report

models.verificationNotes: Confirmed real: Dec 3, 2024 re:Invent launch (Amazon Press Center, TechCrunch, Businesswire all corroborate). Confirmed 300K context, multimodal text/image/video (AWS Nova docs), 200-language support (AWS re:Post + Nova language docs), $0.06/$0.24 per M pricing (matches $0.00006/$0.00024 per 1K on AWS Nova pricing page). All 8 benchmark rows (MMLU 80.5, GPQA 42, MATH 73.3, HumanEval 85.4, GSM8K 94.5, BBH 82.4, IFEval 89.7, DROP 80.2) confirmed to literally appear on deepranking.ai/llm-models/nova-lite. Minor note: deepranking.ai's own page lists a Nov 19, 2024 date for Nova Lite, but all official Amazon sources confirm Dec 3, 2024 — kept the Amazon-sourced date.

models.name: Amazon Nova Micro
models.archDisplay: Transformer, architecture/size undisclosed
models.note: Text-only, lowest-latency/lowest-cost Nova tier; suited to simple tasks and high-throughput agent orchestration.
models.verificationNotes: Confirmed real: Dec 3, 2024 re:Invent launch corroborated by multiple press sources. Confirmed 128K text-only context (AWS Nova docs — Micro is text-to-text only, unlike Lite/Pro/Premier). 200-language support and $0.035/$0.14 per M pricing confirmed against AWS Nova pricing/docs. All 9 benchmark rows (MMLU 77.6, GPQA 40, MATH 69.3, HumanEval 81.1, GSM8K 92.3, BBH 79.5, IFEval 87.2, DROP 79.3, ARC-Challenge 90.2) confirmed to literally appear on deepranking.ai/llm-models/nova-micro. No changes made.

models.name: Amazon Nova Pro
models.archDisplay: Transformer, architecture/size undisclosed
models.note: Highest-accuracy multimodal Nova at launch; workhorse mid/high tier for complex text, image and video tasks before Premier arrived.

results.notes: 0-shot CoT; matches Amazon's Nova technical report

models.verificationNotes: Confirmed real: Dec 3, 2024 re:Invent launch corroborated by multiple press sources. Confirmed 300K multimodal context, 200-language support, $0.80/$3.20 per M pricing (AWS Nova pricing/docs). All 9 benchmark rows (MMLU 85.9, GPQA 46.9, MATH 76.6, HumanEval 89.0, GSM8K 94.8, BBH 86.9, IFEval 92.1, DROP 85.4, ARC-Challenge 94.8) confirmed to literally appear on deepranking.ai/llm-models/nova-pro. No changes made.

models.name: Amazon Nova Premier
models.archDisplay: Transformer, architecture/size undisclosed
models.note: Amazon's most capable Nova: 1M-token context for complex agentic/long-document tasks; explicitly not a reasoning model, positioned mainly as a distillation teacher for smaller Nova models.
models.verificationNotes: Confirmed real: April 30, 2025 launch corroborated by TechCrunch, Computerworld, AWS blog, Amazon Science technical report. Confirmed 1M-token multimodal context, $2.50/$12.50 per M pricing, explicitly described as 'not a reasoning model' and positioned as a distillation teacher (TechCrunch, AWS blog). MMLU-Pro 73.3 and GPQA Diamond 56.9 could not be directly rendered from artificialanalysis.ai/models/nova-premier via automated fetch (client-side rendered page), but both figures were independently corroborated verbatim (73.3% MMLU-Pro, 56.9% GPQA Diamond) on a mirror site (designforonline.com) that sources Artificial Analysis benchmark data. Kept both rows given this independent corroboration.

models.name: abab6.5
models.archDisplay: Mixture-of-Experts, trillion-scale total params (exact count/expert configuration undisclosed)
models.note: MiniMax's first trillion-parameter MoE LLM, targeting GPT-4/Claude-3-class performance at launch; the abab6.5s variant trades some quality for much higher throughput at the same 200K context.
models.verificationNotes: Confirmed real via MiniMax's own news post (minimax.io/news/abab65-series, dated 2024-04-17): 'Abab6.5 features a trillion parameters and supports a context length of 200k tokens; abab6.5s uses the same training techniques and data as abab6.5 but is more efficient.' Confirms MoE architecture, trillion-scale params (undisclosed exact count), 200K context, and the abab6.5/abab6.5s two-variant structure. Function calling/tool use confirmed generally supported by the MiniMax API (parallel function calling, tool selection) per MiniMax API docs. No results rows were present to verify. No changes made.

models.name: MiniMax-Text-01
models.license: MiniMax Model License Agreement (custom, source-available; code under MIT)
models.archDisplay: Hybrid Lightning Attention + Softmax Attention, MoE (32 experts, top-2 routing, 80 layers)
models.note: 456B-param MoE (45.9B active) using Lightning Attention; trained to 1M-token context and extrapolates to 4M at inference; released open-weight alongside vision variant MiniMax-VL-01.
models.tpsNote: MiniMax's own report targets ~640GB memory across 8 GPUs at 8-bit quantization for 1M-token inference; far beyond a single RTX 4090's 24GB, so no consumer-GPU throughput figures exist.
models.verificationNotes: Confirmed real via MarkTechPost (release 2025-01-15), arXiv 2501.08313, GitHub MiniMax-AI/MiniMax-01, and HF model card. 456B total/45.9B active MoE with Lightning Attention, 1M train / 4M inference context all confirmed. All 10 benchmark scores (MMLU 88.5, MMLU-Pro 75.7, GPQA 54.4, DROP 87.8, GSM8K 94.8, MATH 77.4, MBPP+ 71.7, HumanEval 86.9, IFEval 89.1, Arena-Hard 89.1) verified to literally appear on the HF model card (retargeted sourceUrl from arXiv abstract page, which did not render the table, to the HF card which does); no numbers changed.

models.name: MiniMax-VL-01
models.license: Custom MiniMax Model License (free for most use; platforms with >100M MAU must request a separate license; code under MIT)
models.archDisplay: Hybrid Lightning+Softmax Attention MoE (80 layers, 32 experts top-2) + 303M-param ViT/MLP vision encoder
models.note: Vision-language sibling of MiniMax-Text-01, adding a dynamic-resolution (336px-2016px) ViT+MLP encoder atop the same 456B hybrid-attention MoE backbone.
models.verificationNotes: Confirmed real via HF model card (MiniMaxAI/MiniMax-VL-01) and GitHub MiniMax-AI/MiniMax-01. 456B/45.9B MoE backbone + 303M ViT-MLP vision encoder, dynamic resolution 336-2016px all confirmed. All 4 benchmark scores (MMMU 68.5, MathVista 68.6, DocVQA 96.4, ChartQA 91.7) confirmed literally on the HF card. Price ($0.2 input / $1.1 output per 1M tokens) independently confirmed via current MiniMax pricing pages. No changes needed.

models.name: MiniMax-M1
models.archDisplay: Hybrid Lightning+Softmax Attention MoE (80 layers, 32 experts top-2) reasoning model, trained with CISPO RL

price.provider: MiniMax (platform.minimax.io, 0-200K token input tier; 200K-1M tier is $1.3/$2.2)

models.note: World's first open-weight large-scale hybrid-attention reasoning model; shipped as two checkpoints (40K/80K thinking budget) - this entry reflects the flagship 80K checkpoint.
models.tpsNote: llama.cpp did not support the MiniMaxM1ForCausalLM architecture at release, so no GGUF/Q4 quant or RTX 4090 figure exists.
models.verificationNotes: Confirmed real via VentureBeat, Computerworld, arXiv 2506.13585, and HF model card MiniMaxAI/MiniMax-M1-80k. 456B/45.9B hybrid MoE, Apache-2.0, 1M context confirmed by multiple independent sources. All 5 result rows confirmed literally on the HF card, including the M1-80K vs M1-40K split (AIME 86.0, LCB 65.0, SWE-bench 56.0 for 80K; GPQA 69.2, MMLU-Pro 80.6 for 40K) — exact match, no changes. Pricing $0.4/$2.2 for 0-200K tier independently confirmed.

models.name: MiniMax-M2
models.archDisplay: 62-layer decoder-only Transformer, 256 experts (top-8, sigmoid gating) + Multi-Token Prediction module, full GQA attention
models.note: Compact 229.9B-total/9.8B-active MoE tuned for coding and agentic workflows with interleaved <think> reasoning; original M2 release is MIT-licensed (later M2.x point releases, e.g. M2.1/M2.7, moved to a restrictive 'Modified-MIT').
models.tpsNote: Unsloth's UD-IQ4_XS dynamic GGUF (~108GB) is reported at ~15-25 tok/s on a single 16GB GPU + 96GB system RAM or Mac unified memory - no RTX-4090-specific figure found.
models.verificationNotes: Confirmed real; official minimax.io/news/minimax-m2 post is literally dated 2025.10.27, matching releaseDate exactly (MarkTechPost/Simon Willison coverage followed a day or two later). 229.9B total/9.8B active MIT-licensed MoE confirmed via GitHub repo and MarkTechPost. All 7 benchmark rows (SWE-bench 69.4, LiveCodeBench 83, MMLU-Pro 82, AIME25 78, Terminal-Bench 46.3, GAIA 75.7, HLE 31.8) confirmed literally on the GitHub README. Independently confirmed the note's claim that later M2.x point releases (M2.1, M2.7) carry a distinct 'Modified-MIT' license requiring UI attribution, unlike the base M2's plain MIT.

models.name: Baichuan2-13B
models.license: Apache-2.0 (code) + Baichuan2 Community License for weights (free commercial use if DAU<1M, non-cloud-provider, no sublicensing)
models.archDisplay: Dense decoder-only Transformer with ALiBi positional bias, 40 layers
models.note: Flagship of the Baichuan 2 open bilingual (zh/en) series, trained on 2.6T tokens with ALiBi attention; released as Base+Chat+4bit with a full technical report.
models.verificationNotes: Confirmed real via arXiv 2309.10305, MarkTechPost (2023-09-19), and the official GitHub README_EN.md. All 6 benchmark scores (C-Eval 58.1, MMLU 59.17, CMMLU 61.97, GSM8K 52.77, HumanEval 17.07, BBH 48.78) confirmed to literally appear in the README table for the 13B-Base model. License (Apache-2.0 code + custom community license with DAU<1M free-commercial-use clause) confirmed. Context length 4096 (4K) confirmed via Ollama model metadata; retargeted ctxK sourceUrl from an unrelated HF text-generation-inference PR to a source that actually states the figure. Corrected hf link from Baichuan2-13B-Chat (was mistakenly Base in original links.hf field is now Chat, matching the Chat/4bit release most commonly used).

models.name: Baichuan2-7B
models.license: Apache-2.0 (code) + Baichuan2 Community License for weights (free commercial use if DAU<1M, non-cloud-provider, no sublicensing)
models.archDisplay: Dense decoder-only Transformer with RoPE, 32 layers
models.note: Early strong bilingual (zh/en) open LLM trained on 2.6T tokens; released as Base+Chat with a full technical report and 4bit quantized variant.
models.verificationNotes: Confirmed real via arXiv 2309.10305, MarkTechPost (2023-09-19), and the official GitHub README_EN.md. All 5 benchmark scores (C-Eval 54.0, MMLU 54.16, CMMLU 57.07, GSM8K 24.49, HumanEval 18.29) confirmed to literally appear in the README table for the 7B-Base model. License and bilingual (zh/en) claim confirmed. Context length 4096 (4K) confirmed via Ollama model metadata; retargeted ctxK sourceUrl to a source that actually states the figure.

models.name: Baichuan 3
models.archDisplay: Undisclosed architecture; vendor describes total parameters as exceeding one trillion

price.provider: Baichuan (platform.baichuan-ai.com, blended CNY rate converted to USD)

models.note: Trillion-parameter-class closed model claimed to beat GPT-4 on CMMLU/Gaokao/AGIEval and medical exams (MCMLE/MedExam/CMExam); exact parameter count undisclosed.
models.verificationNotes: Confirmed real: independent search of Pandaily's article corroborates a Jan 29 2024 release with parameters 'exceeding one trillion', matching the record exactly. No result rows were present to verify. paramsB left null (genuinely undisclosed beyond the '>1T' claim); price/openness left unchanged as no contradicting evidence was found (platform pricing page not independently re-fetched due to search-tool rate limiting).

models.name: Baichuan 4
models.archDisplay: Undisclosed architecture; closed flagship with multimodal (MMMU/MMBench/MathVista) capability

price.provider: Baichuan (platform.baichuan-ai.com, blended CNY rate converted to USD)

models.note: Closed flagship launched with the 'Baixiaoying' AI assistant; topped SuperCLUE (80.64) above GPT-4-Turbo-0125 and Claude 3 Opus on Chinese-language tasks at release.

results.notes: SuperCLUE comprehensive Chinese benchmark, surpassed GPT-4-Turbo-0125

models.verificationNotes: Confirmed real: braintitan.medium.com independently corroborates May 22 2024 release date, matching record. The inf.news sourceUrl returned HTTP 403 on direct fetch, but a fresh web search that crawled the same URL surfaced the literal text 'Baichuan 4 ranked first with a score of 80.64' on that page, confirming the SuperCLUE row. Kept as-is.

models.name: Inflection-1
models.archDisplay: Dense Transformer (architecture, dataset, and training procedure undisclosed)
models.note: Pi's original in-house model; GPT-3.5-class personal-AI chatbot tuned for empathetic conversation over coding or reasoning.

results.notes: Average across 57 MMLU tasks; outperformed GPT-3.5, LLaMA, and PaLM-540B per Inflection's technical memo.

models.verificationNotes: Confirmed real via direct fetch of inflection.ai/inflection-1: page literally states '72.7% on average across all 57 tasks' on MMLU, matching the score exactly. Fetch also confirmed the June 22 2023 announcement date. No changes made.

models.name: Inflection-2
models.archDisplay: Dense Transformer (architecture undisclosed; trained at ~10^25 FLOPs)
models.note: Trained on 5,000 H100 GPUs at ~10^25 FLOPs; billed at launch as the second-most-capable LLM in the world, beating PaLM 2 Large.

results.notes: Independent comparison table; Inflection-2 ranked 2nd behind GPT-4 (86.4), just above Claude-2 CoT (78.5). Literally confirmed on page.
10-shot HellaSwag. Literally confirmed: page text states 'Inflection-2 reaches 89.0 on HellaSwag 10-shot compared to GPT-4's 95.3.'

models.verificationNotes: Confirmed model is real; training-completion/announcement dated Nov 22-28 2023 across sources (Forbes article dated Nov 22, 2023), consistent with record. DROPPED three result rows that could not be confirmed as literal text on the cited inflection.ai/inflection-2 page: self-reported MMLU 86.8, GSM8K 89.3, and TriviaQA 86.3. Direct full-text fetch of that page (twice) found these benchmark tables are presented only as chart IMAGES — the numeric strings '86.8', '89.3', and '86.3' do not appear anywhere in the page's actual text/markdown, and no alternate source could locate a text-form citation of these exact figures. Kept HellaSwag 89 (literally stated as '89.0' in body text) and independent MMLU 79.6 (literally confirmed on searchenginejournal.com's comparison table).

models.name: Inflection-2.5
models.archDisplay: Dense Transformer (architecture undisclosed)
models.note: Reached ~94% of GPT-4's average IQ-benchmark performance using only 40% of GPT-4's training compute; Inflection's last flagship before the March 2024 Microsoft talent exodus.

results.notes: Aggregate performance vs GPT-4 across Inflection's IQ suite (MMLU, GPQA Diamond, BIG-Bench-Hard, MBPP+/HumanEval+, HellaSwag, ARC-C); per-benchmark numbers were published only as chart images, not text, so exact figures are unavailable.

models.verificationNotes: Confirmed real via direct fetch of inflection.ai/inflection-2-5: page literally states 'Inflection-2.5, now powering Pi, achieves more than 94% the average performance of GPT-4 despite using only 40% the training FLOPs,' matching the record exactly, and confirms March 7 2024 publish date. No changes made.

models.name: Llama-3.3-Nemotron-Super-49B v1 (Reasoning Off)
models.license: NVIDIA Open Model License + Llama 3.3 Community License Agreement
models.archDisplay: NAS-derived dense Transformer (Llama-3.3-70B lineage; skip-attention & variable-FFN blocks)
models.note: NAS-compressed Llama-3.3-70B reasoning model with a toggleable 'detailed thinking on/off' system-prompt switch; optimized to fit a single H100-80GB at high throughput.
models.tpsNote: Q4 (~30GB) exceeds a single RTX 4090's 24GB VRAM; NVIDIA targets a single H100/H200-80GB GPU instead.

results.notes: Reasoning off. Literally confirmed on HF model card.
MATH500, reasoning off. Literally confirmed on HF model card.
AIME25, reasoning off. Literally confirmed on HF model card.
reasoning off. Literally confirmed on HF model card.
Strict, reasoning off. Literally confirmed on HF model card.
0-shot, reasoning off. Literally confirmed on HF model card.
reasoning off. Literally confirmed on HF model card.
reasoning off. Literally confirmed on HF model card.

models.verificationNotes: Confirmed real via direct fetch of the HF model card: release date March 18 2025, ~49-50B params (HF UI badge rounds to '50B params'; NVIDIA's own naming and docs use 49B, kept as record states), NVIDIA Open Model License + Llama 3.3 Community License, 128K context, and English + 7 other languages (German/French/Italian/Portuguese/Hindi/Spanish/Thai = 8 total, matching langCount). All 8 'reasoning off' benchmark scores (Arena-Hard 88.3, MATH500 74, AIME25 13.33, GPQA 50, IFEval 89.21, MBPP 84.9, BFCL-v2-live 73.7, MT-Bench 9.17) were independently re-fetched and literally match the HF page. GGUF/FP8/MLX quant availability confirmed via existing bartowski/nvidia/mlx-community repos. Single-H100/H200 deployment framing confirmed via NVIDIA-sourced descriptions. No changes made.

models.name: Llama-3.3-Nemotron-Super-49B v1 (Reasoning On)
models.license: NVIDIA Open Model License + Llama 3.3 Community License Agreement
models.archDisplay: NAS-derived dense Transformer (Llama-3.3-70B lineage; skip-attention & variable-FFN blocks)
models.note: Same weights as the reasoning-off configuration, activated via a 'detailed thinking on' system prompt with temperature 0.6/top_p 0.95 for chain-of-thought decoding.
models.tpsNote: Q4 (~30GB) exceeds a single RTX 4090's 24GB VRAM; NVIDIA targets a single H100/H200-80GB GPU instead.
models.verificationNotes: Confirmed real via NVIDIA's official HF model card (fetched directly). All specs match: 49B dense NAS-derived model from Llama-3.3-70B, 128K context, NVIDIA Open Model License + Llama 3.3 Community License, released 2025-03-18. All 4 result rows (MATH500 96.6, AIME25 58.4, GPQA 66.67, MBPP 91.3) literally confirmed in the reasoning-on column of the model card's benchmark table. No changes needed.

models.name: Llama-3.1-Nemotron-Ultra-253B v1 (Reasoning Off)
models.license: NVIDIA Open Model License + Llama 3.1 Community License Agreement
models.archDisplay: NAS-derived dense Transformer (Llama-3.1-405B lineage; skip-attention & variable-FFN blocks)
models.note: NAS-derived flagship reasoning model distilled from Llama-3.1-405B; briefly the most intelligent open model, outperforming DeepSeek-R1 at roughly half the size.
models.tpsNote: 253B parameters (FP16 ~506GB, Q4 ~127GB) require a multi-GPU node such as 8xH100; far beyond a single RTX 4090.
models.verificationNotes: Confirmed real via NVIDIA's official HF model card (fetched directly). 253B dense NAS-derived model from Llama-3.1-405B, 128K context, correct license, released 2025-04-07. All 6 result rows in the reasoning-off column (GPQA 56.6, AIME25 16.67, MATH500 80.4, LiveCodeBench 29.03, IFEval 88.85, BFCL-v2-live 73.62) literally confirmed on the model card. No changes needed.

models.name: Llama-3.1-Nemotron-Ultra-253B v1 (Reasoning On)
models.license: NVIDIA Open Model License + Llama 3.1 Community License Agreement
models.archDisplay: NAS-derived dense Transformer (Llama-3.1-405B lineage; skip-attention & variable-FFN blocks)
models.note: Reasoning-on configuration (chain-of-thought via <think> tags) beat DeepSeek-R1 on GPQA (76.0 vs 71.5), IFEval (89.5 vs 83.3) and LiveCodeBench (66.3 vs 65.9) at roughly 4x the inference throughput.
models.tpsNote: 253B parameters (FP16 ~506GB, Q4 ~127GB) require a multi-GPU node such as 8xH100; far beyond a single RTX 4090.

results.notes: reasoning on; DeepSeek-R1 scored 71.5 per VentureBeat's comparison
reasoning on; DeepSeek-R1 scored 65.9 per VentureBeat
reasoning on; DeepSeek-R1 scored 83.3 per VentureBeat

models.verificationNotes: Confirmed real via NVIDIA's official HF model card (fetched directly), same page as reasoning-off variant. All 6 result rows in the reasoning-on column (GPQA 76.01, AIME25 72.5, MATH500 97, LiveCodeBench 66.31, IFEval 89.45, BFCL-v2-live 74.1) literally confirmed on the model card. DeepSeek-R1 comparison figures in notes are contextual claims from VentureBeat, not independently re-verified against a DeepSeek source, but the underlying Nemotron scores themselves are confirmed. No changes needed.

models.name: Dolphin 2.5 Mixtral 8x7B
models.archDisplay: Sparse Mixture-of-Experts (8 experts, top-2 routing; Mixtral 8x7B base)
models.note: QLoRA fine-tune of Mixtral 8x7B (Apache-2.0) with refusals filtered out and Dolphin-Coder/MagiCoder data added; a benchmark of the late-2023 'uncensored' MoE fine-tune wave.
models.tpsNote: Q4 (~26GB) slightly exceeds a single RTX 4090's 24GB VRAM; typically run with partial CPU offload or reduced context.
models.verificationNotes: Confirmed real: HF repo dphn/dolphin-2.5-mixtral-8x7b exists, fine-tune of Mixtral-8x7B by Eric Hartford / Cognitive Computations, Apache-2.0, base 32K context fine-tuned at 16K, trained with Dolphin-Coder/MagiCoder data and refusals filtered (confirmed on model card and erichartford.com blog). releaseDate corrected/confirmed precisely via HF API createdAt field = 2023-12-14T00:45:47Z, matching the record exactly (training itself ran Dec 9-11, 2023, blog post published Dec 22, 2023). No results rows were present to verify. No changes needed.

models.name: Snowflake Arctic
models.archDisplay: Dense-MoE Hybrid (10B dense transformer + 128x3.66B residual MoE experts, top-2 gating)
models.note: Apache-2.0 480B-total/17B-active dense+MoE hybrid (128 experts) tuned for enterprise SQL/code/instruction-following, trained for under $2M.
models.tpsNote: 480B total parameters (Q4 ~291GB) require a multi-GPU server; not runnable on a single consumer GPU, and mainline llama.cpp needs a community fork for Arctic's architecture.
models.verificationNotes: Confirmed real via Snowflake's official blog (fetched directly): 480B total / 17B active dense+MoE hybrid (10B dense + 128x3.66B experts, top-2 gating), Apache-2.0, released 2024-04-24, trained for under $2M. Context length of 4K confirmed directly from the HF config.json max_position_embeddings=4096 field. All 4 result rows (MMLU 67.3, GSM8k 74.2, HumanEval 64.3, IFEval 52.4) literally confirmed present on the cited Flexera blog page. No changes needed.
